1.gl: properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. to be eminent (as a superintendent, especially of the Temple services and its music); to be permanent
1.kj: excel, chief musician (singer), oversee(-r), set forward.
1.dv: a primitive root;  also as denominative from H5331 (נֶצַח)

1.gl: David, the youngest son of Jesse
1.dv: rarely (fully); דָּוִיד; from the same as H1730 (דּוֹד); loving;

1.gl: properly, instrumental music; by implication, a poem set to notes

2.gl: to hear intelligently (often with implication of attention, obedience, etc.; causatively, to tell, etc.)

2.gl: alive; hence, raw (flesh); fresh (plant, water, year), strong; also (as noun, especially in the feminine singular and masculine plural) life (or living thing), whether literally or figuratively

2.gl: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative
2.kj: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.
[phrase] aloud, bleating, crackling, cry ([phrase] out), fame, lightness, lowing, noise, [phrase] hold peace, (pro-) claim, proclamation, [phrase] sing, sound, [phrase] spark, thun
2.dv: or קֹל; from an unused root meaning to call aloud;

2.gl: to guard, in a good sense (to protect, maintain, obey, etc.) or a bad one (to conceal, etc.)
2.kj: besieged, hidden thing, keep(-er, -ing), monument, observe, preserve(-r), subtil, watcher(-man).

2.gl: a (sudden) alarm (properly, the object feared, by implication, the feeling)

3.gl: properly, to spoil (literally, by breaking to pieces); figuratively, to make (or be) good fornothing, i.e. bad (physically, socially or morally)

3.gl: strictly nothingness; also trouble. vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol
3.kj: affliction, evil, false, idol, iniquity, mischief, mourners(-ing), naught, sorrow, unjust, unrighteous, vain, vanity, wicked(-ness). Compare H369 (אַיִן).
3.dv: from an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant (hence, to exert oneself, usually in vain; to come to naught);

3.gl: a session, i.e. company of persons (in close deliberation); by implication, intimacy, consultation, a secret
to hide (by covering), literally or figuratively
3.kj: be absent, keep close, conceal, hide (self), (keep) secret, [idiom] surely.

3.gl: to do or make (systematically and habitually), especially to practise
